title: Cookies, Authentication, Private Data, Write Avoidance, and Session Cleanup · Runtime Lens: Session Cleanup Evidence
summary: A crawler logs in with a personal account, stores cookies in logs, follows logout and mutation links, and exports fields beyond the authorized purpose. Predict authorization, identity, scope, parsing, extraction, crawl state, resource use, privacy, accessibility, failure, and evidence before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the session cleanup evidence increment in authorized session and data-custody contract. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory,…
objectives: Close sessions, erase transient credentials and storage, record revocation or logout limits, and verify logs and artifacts contain no secrets.
Filter, redact, aggregate, encrypt, retain, and delete private records according to the authorized purpose before export.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-web-scraper-python cumulative authorized crawler and accessible site-audit portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python policy, URL, response, tree, frontier, record,
and report models with original fixed in-memory fixtures only. Sockets, DNS, public or private
sites, credentials, host files, native HTTPX, Beautiful Soup, lxml, Scrapy, Playwright browsers,
accessibility claims, legal review, load, schedules, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class CrawlPolicy:
    owner_authorization_id: str
    allowed_origins: tuple[str, ...]
    maximum_pages: int = 240
    maximum_response_bytes: int = 2_000_000
    minimum_origin_spacing_seconds: float = 1.0


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class CrawlEvidence:
    revision: str
    policy_digest: str
    frontier_digest: str
    report_digest: str
    accessibility_review_id: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Authorized Python crawler portfolio ready"

content.code:
# Worked focus: scraper-session-cleanup-evidence — Close sessions, erase transient credentials and storage, record revocation or logout limits, and verify logs and artifacts contain no secrets.
# Evidence: scraper-worked-scraper-session-auth-boundaries-2
# Competency: scraper-worked-scraper-session-auth-boundaries-2.
# Case 7417: a privacy-minimized internal crawl.
# Operating constraint: the prior valid checkpoint and report must survive failure.
# Changed case: change one base URL, fragment, query, Unicode host, or redirect and reapply scope.
def worked_scraper_session_auth_boundaries_2(authorized_pages: int = 24, collected_pages: int = 24, retained_private_fields: int = 4, approved_private_fields: int = 4, leaked_secrets: int = 0) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_worked2 = "worked2-2dekrow-7417"
    assert evidence_variant_worked2
    evidence_axes_worked2 = (
        "authorizationworked2",
        "url7417",
        "response2dekrow",
        "treeworked27417",
        "frontier7417worked2",
        "report2dekrow7417",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_worked2)) == 6
    if authorized_pages < 1 or collected_pages < 0:
        return False, collected_pages, "session-count"
    if collected_pages != authorized_pages:
        return False, collected_pages, "authorization-scope"
    if retained_private_fields != approved_private_fields:
        return False, retained_private_fields, "private-field-scope"
    if leaked_secrets > 0:
        return False, leaked_secrets, "secret-leak"
    assert collected_pages == authorized_pages and leaked_secrets == 0
    return True, collected_pages, "authorized-session-custody"
content.caption: Runnable deterministic fixed-fixture pure-Python crawler decision model; trace one accepted and one rejected authorization, URL, response, tree, record, frontier, browser, report, or recovery case, then state which network, owner, robots, HTTPX, Beautiful Soup, lxml, Scrapy, Playwright, accessibility, privacy, security, legal, load, schedule, or production claim still needs controlled transfer evidence.

content.code:
# Evidence: scraper-session-cleanup-evidence-1x1tug
# Competency: scraper-session-cleanup-evidence.
# Case 6697: a privacy-minimized internal crawl.
# Operating constraint: automated findings must not claim ranking or WCAG conformance.
# Changed case: change one base URL, fragment, query, Unicode host, or redirect and reapply scope.
def evidence_scraper_session_cleanup_evidence_1x1tug(authorized_pages: int = 24, collected_pages: int = 24, retained_private_fields: int = 4, approved_private_fields: int = 4, leaked_secrets: int = 0) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_1x1tug = "1x1tug-gut1x1-6697"
    assert evidence_variant_1x1tug
    evidence_axes_1x1tug = (
        "authorization1x1tug",
        "url6697",
        "responsegut1x1",
        "tree1x1tug6697",
        "frontier66971x1tug",
        "reportgut1x16697",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_1x1tug)) == 6
    if authorized_pages < 1 or collected_pages < 0:
        return False, collected_pages, "session-count"
    if collected_pages != authorized_pages:
        return False, collected_pages, "authorization-scope"
    if retained_private_fields != approved_private_fields:
        return False, retained_private_fields, "private-field-scope"
    if leaked_secrets > 0:
        return False, leaked_secrets, "secret-leak"
    assert collected_pages == authorized_pages and leaked_secrets == 0
    return True, collected_pages, "authorized-session-custody"

stimulus.caption: Three excerpts from a learner evidence packet; only one connects a claim to a reproducible check.

lines.label: assumption
lines.text: A theory site-quality team handles case 6030 during a multilingual public-service site review. Build deterministic Python evidence to reconcile cookie and credential scope, safe retrieval, private-data minimization, and session cleanup; automated findings must not claim ranking or WCAG conformance; then replace a static fixture with an explicitly authorized dynamic case and restate the browser budget. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real DNS, sockets, TLS, HTTPX, Beautiful Soup, lxml, Scrapy, Playwright, browsers, files, credentials, target sites, accessibility evaluation, privacy or legal review, load, schedulers, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Close sessions, erase transient credentials and storage, record revocation or logout limits, and verify logs and artifacts contain no secrets.

lines.label: risk
lines.text: Process exit reliably clears every credential and browser storage artifact.

lines.label: evidence
lines.text: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Close sessions, erase transient credentials and storage, record revocation or logout limits, and verify logs and artifacts contain no secrets. Artifact under review: authorized session and data-custody contract.

options.text: The Session Cleanup Evidence record says authorized session and data-custody contract looks right but supplies no governing condition, observation, changed case, or counterexample. That claim fails the inspect session cleanup evidence boundary for Session Cleanup Evidence.

For inspect session cleanup evidence, crawl one convenient page, omit owner authorization, revision, URL and robots policy, response budgets, parser and selector limits, provenance, frontier and concurrency evidence, changed fixtures, privacy, security, accessibility, stop, recovery, and controlled native transfer, then accept plausible rows as crawler proof. This does not satisfy Session Cleanup Evidence for authorized session and data-custody contract.
The record names the assumption, applies Session Cleanup Evidence, and reports a changed-case check tied to the stakeholder decision.

steps.title: Authorized crawling and Python Defend the Session Cleanup Evidence model · Theory Session Cleanup E…
steps.instruction:
Write the theory evidence note for Session Cleanup Evidence: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A theory site-quality team handles case 4411 during a privacy-minimized internal crawl. Build deterministic Python evidence to reconcile cookie and credential scope, safe retrieval, private-data minimization, and session cleanup; the prior valid checkpoint and report must survive failure; then change a robots, canonical, indexing, structured-data, or accessibility signal and retain the conflict. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real DNS, sockets, TLS, HTTPX, Beautiful Soup, lxml, Scrapy, Playwright, browsers, files, credentials, target sites, accessibility evaluation, privacy or legal review, load, schedulers, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Close sessions, erase transient credentials and storage, record revocation or logout limits, and verify logs and artifacts contain no secrets.

Evidence target: Authorized crawling and Python Defend the Session Cleanup Evidence model · Theory Session Cleanup E….
steps.why: Retrieving Session Cleanup Evidence through the concrete authorized session and data-custody contract case exposes causal gaps that recognition alone can hide. For Authorized crawling and Python Defend the Session Cleanup Evidence model · Theory Session Cleanup E…, keep Session Cleanup Evidence connected to executable Python authorization, URL, response, parse, provenance, frontier, report, and recovery decision evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Close sessions, erase transient credentials and storage, record revocation or logout limits, and verify logs and artifacts contain no secrets.

hints: For Authorized crawling and Python Defend the Session Cleanup Evidence model · Theory Session Cleanup E…, name the owner, authorization, stakeholder, revision, runtime, dependency, seed, URL, origin, robots, response, parser, selector, record, frontier, privacy, report, stop, recovery, and transfer boundary, Session Cleanup Evidence precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Authorized crawling and Python Defend the Session Cleanup Evidence model · Theory Session Cleanup E…, trace this concrete input and predict authorization, URL admission, robots, fetch, redirect, decode, parse, extract, provenance, schedule, report, rejection, stop, rollback, recovery, and native evidence before reviewing it: A theory site-quality team handles case 4411 during a privacy-minimized internal crawl. Build deterministic Python evidence to reconcile cookie and credential scope, safe retrieval, private-data minimization, and session cleanup; the prior valid checkpoint and report must survive failure; then change a robots, canonical, indexing, structured-data, or accessibility signal and retain the conflict. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real DNS, sockets, TLS, HTTPX, Beautiful Soup, lxml, Scrapy, Playwright, browsers, files, credentials, target sites, accessibility evaluation, privacy or legal review, load, schedulers, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Close sessions, erase transient credentials and storage, record revocation or logout limits, and verify logs and artifacts contain no secrets.

For Authorized crawling and Python Defend the Session Cleanup Evidence model · Theory Session Cleanup E…, challenge the documented misconception with changed-authorization, URL, robots rule, response, encoding, markup, selector, frontier, timing, session, browser, privacy, security, export, failure, recovery, and controlled owner transfer evidence: Process exit reliably clears every credential and browser storage artifact.

checks.description: The cumulative python portfolio contains this competency’s named evidence increment.

checks.pattern: # Evidence: scraper-session-cleanup-evidence-1x1tug(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?def\s+evidence_scraper_session_cleanup_evidence_1x1tug\s*\((?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?\)\s*(?:->[^:]+)?\s*:(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?authorized_pages < 1 or collected_pages < 0)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?collected_pages != authorized_pages)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?retained_private_fields != approved_private_fields)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?leaked_secrets > 0)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?assert)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?return)(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?return
